stat: median per-embed, end-to-end (wav -> embedding), contended box (load ~13)
ram_method: peak resident set size from /usr/bin/time -v (Maximum resident set size), median of 5 verify runs on this box. ggml = voicedetect-cli verify (mmap'd GGUF, zero Python). onnxruntime = the reference verify path (soundfile + torchaudio Kaldi-FBank + onnxruntime CPU). Measured against the CPU-only torch wheel (the fair CPU-to-CPU comparison): ggml 61.4 MB vs 334.0 MB, ~5.4x. The default CUDA torch wheel would show ~11x but loads unused CUDA libs into RSS even on a CPU verify, so we report the conservative CPU-to-CPU number.
honesty_note: End-to-end the two engines are within ~12 percent; onnxruntime model inference alone (10.63 ms) is faster, voice-detect.cpp reaches parity via a faster integrated FBank. By min, onnxruntime is faster (8.59 vs 11.88). NOT a 1.51x win. Durable wins: bit-exact parity, zero-Python single binary, and ~5.4x less peak RAM (61.4 vs 334.0 MB, CPU-only torch; the default CUDA torch wheel would show ~11x).
